evidence_summary: Belarus Free Theatre co-founder Natalya Kolyada and ghost historian Jack Sim Broadcast date: Thursday 22 January 2009 Broadcast date: Thursday 22 January 2009 Co-founder Belarus Free Theatre Natalya Kolyada Natalya Kolyada is a human rights activist from Belarus. She and her husband founded the Belarus Free Theatre in 2005, and they hold performances in their home country in private apartments or in forests, hiding from the authorities. They are able to perform - to great acclaim - internationally, and touring Australia with their production of Being Harold Pinter. The Belarus Free Theatre are in Brisbane this week, at the Powerhouse (until Saturday 24th January). Ghost historian and author Jack Sim Jack Sim spends many of his days, and nights,.

title: Australia's Most Haunted
australian_relation: Exact ABC public media items naming Queensland and New South Wales haunted towns, gaols, and ghost-story sites.
coordinate_evidence_note: ABC text names `Monte Cristo Homestead` in a ghost/supernatural context; gazetteer result state-checked as Monte Cristo Homestead, 1, Homestead Lane, Junee North, Junee, New South Wales, 2663, Australia.
evidence_summary: Australia's Most Haunted Tim the Yowie Man shares 15 years of investigation into the most mysterious and haunted places in Australia. Tim the Yowie Man shares 15 years of investigation into the most mysterious and haunted places in Australia. Rachael Kohn: The Monte Cristo Homestead in Junee, New South Wales, is the most haunted house in Australia, according to Tim the Yowie Man. This is The Ark, on ABC Radio National, with me, Rachael Kohn. This week, 'The Steve Irwin of Spooks' tells us some tall tales from the city to the outback. Monte Cristo is just one of the places people feel a shiver going up their spine: phantom piano playing, apparitions at the foot of the bed, a chill in the air. They've all been reported. But spooks don't just scare.
